sanskrit:
सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम्।
सर्वतः श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति॥
transliteration: sarvataḥ pāṇi-pādaṃ tat sarvato-'kṣi-śiro-mukham | sarvataḥ śrutimal loke sarvam āvṛtya tiṣṭhati ||
literal_meaning: Everywhere hands and feet, everywhere eyes, heads and faces, everywhere ears, pervading everything in the world it stands.
contextual_meaning: The field-knower (kṣetrajña) is not confined to a single body-mind; it is the omnipresent awareness that simultaneously perceives every point in space-time. Psychologically, this invites the client to shift identity from the limited ego-bound self to the panoramic witness, reducing existential isolation and hyper-vigilance.
psychological_state: Expansion from personal to trans-personal awareness; dissolving body-map boundaries while retaining grounded safety.
trigger_scenario: A client with panic disorder feels every corner of the room is 'watching' them; reframing this as the benevolent omnipresence of awareness itself turns threat into therapeutic containment.
anchor_text: I am the aware space in which every sight, sound and sensation appears.
